name: review-depth-async
author: diffscope
description: Async-correctness benchmark pack for deeper live review runs.

fixtures.name: rust-fire-and-forget-sync-task

expected_findings.description: Detached background task cannot be awaited or monitored by the caller.
contains_any: errors are never returned to the caller
errors can never be returned to the caller

expected_findings.description: Background task unwrap discards sync failures instead of propagating or logging them.
contains_any: returns ok even when sync_user fails

fixtures.description: Async work should not be detached if the caller relies on success or error propagation.

fixtures.name: typescript-missing-await-permission-check

expected_findings.description: Missing await makes the permission check always truthy and bypasses authorization.

fixtures.description: Awaiting async permission checks is required before destructive actions.

fixtures.name: typescript-async-foreach-not-awaited

expected_findings.description: Async work launched inside forEach is not awaited before the function returns.

fixtures.description: Notification fan-out should keep awaiting all spawned async work.

fixtures.name: rust-blocking-in-async-runtime

expected_findings.description: Blocking filesystem I/O is performed inside async runtime work instead of using Tokio's async APIs or spawn_blocking.

fixtures.description: Async Rust code should avoid blocking std::fs work on runtime threads.

fixtures.name: python-asyncio-run-in-running-loop
fixtures.diff_content:
diff --git a/app/tasks.py b/app/tasks.py
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/app/tasks.py
+++ b/app/tasks.py
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
 async def refresh_reports():
-    await sync_reports()
+    asyncio.run(sync_reports())
     return {"ok": True}

expected_findings.description: asyncio.run is called from inside an async function where an event loop is already running.

fixtures.description: Python async functions should not start a fresh event loop with asyncio.run.

fixtures.name: go-context-cancel-defer-order
fixtures.diff_content:
diff --git a/service/worker.go b/service/worker.go
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/service/worker.go
+++ b/service/worker.go
@@ -1,7 +1,8 @@
 func StartWorker(parent context.Context) error {
     ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(parent, time.Second)
     go runWorker(ctx)
-    defer cancel()
+    cancel()
+    defer cancel()
     return nil
 }

expected_findings.description: Context is canceled immediately after spawning the goroutine, so the worker may observe a canceled context before it starts useful work.
contains_any: goroutine may start with a canceled context

fixtures.description: Go concurrency code should not cancel shared contexts before spawned work has a chance to run.
